title: A Flute Of Marvel
content:
Under the leaves and cool flowers
    The wind brought me the sound of a flute
    From far away.

    I cut a branch of willow
    And answered with a lazy song.

    Even at night, when all slept,
    The birds were listening to a conversation
    In their own language.

    From the Chinese of Li Po (705-763).
meaning:
Edward Powys Mathers (As Translator)'s contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "A Flute Of Marvel"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
